title: Ampelocissus floccosa
taxonomy: Ampelocissus floccosa is classified under the family Vitaceae and the order Rhamnales. It is a species of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The classification of this plant is also supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its woody vines and small, white flowers. Further classification places it within the genus Ampelocissus, which includes other species of climbing plants. The classification of Ampelocissus floccosa is also supported by genetic studies that confirm its relationship to other members of the Vitaceae family.

intro: Ampelocissus floccosa, also known as the Himalayan grape or Indian grapevine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Vitaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ampelocissus floccosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ampelocissus floccosa is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, and is used to make various herbal remedies, including infusions, tonics, and ointments, to treat a range of health conditions.
